title: malvastrum bicuspidatum subsp. bicuspidatum
intro: Malvastrum bicuspidatum subsp. bicuspidatum, commonly known as bushmallow or desert hollyhock, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Malvastrum bicuspidatum subsp. bicuspidatum has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's health benefits include treating wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as providing relief from fever and sore throats. The herbal preparations of Malvastrum bicuspidatum subsp. bicuspidatum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
